description_long: This fence consists of two sturdy, weathered wooden posts connected by a single thick rope, which is loosely draped between them. The wood appears aged and rough, with visible grain, knots, and some moss or lichen growth, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The rope is thick, twisted, and also shows signs of wear, with a greenish tint likely from moss or algae. The posts are unevenly cut at the top, and the rope is tied securely around each post with a simple knot. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments, giving the fence a rustic, utilitarian appearance. This type of fence is commonly used in parks, gardens, or along pathways to demarcate boundaries without fully obstructing passage.
description: A rustic fence made of two weathered wooden posts connected by a thick, mossy rope. The design is simple and utilitarian, with visible aging and no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wooden posts and the full length of the rope draped between them.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of one post and the rope, with the other post mostly hidden.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, showing both posts and the rope from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the other post in the foreground.
